title: pouteria martinicensis
intro: Pouteria martinicensis, also known as the West Indian Mahogany or White Lauroy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of digestive problems. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be made by boiling the leaves, bark, or roots of the plant in water to create a medicinal tea or poultice.
